headline: Telemedicine Consultations Reach 2.8 Million But Regulation Gaps Persist
context: Telemedicine use surged post-COVID but regulatory framework for virtual healthcare remains incomplete.

cards.big: 2.8 million telemedicine consultations in 2025 but no comprehensive regulatory framework exists
cards.sub: Virtual medical practice operates in regulatory grey zone 5 years after COVID acceleration

cards.big: Telemedicine Act 1997 predates modern digital health and has not been substantively updated
cards.sub: 28-year-old legislation cannot address contemporary telehealth service models

cards.big: 14 telemedicine platforms operate in Malaysia with no standardized quality certification
cards.sub: Patient safety standards vary widely between platforms without oversight

cards.big: Singapore's Healthtech Regulatory Sandbox tested 45 telemedicine innovations with patient safeguards
cards.sub: Structured regulatory experimentation enables innovation while protecting consumers

cards.big: Telemedicine without regulation is not innovation—it is uncontrolled experimentation on patients
Should Malaysia update telemedicine legislation to include quality standards and patient protections
